title: The Old Man and Death
lines: An Old Man, tired and worn from labor, sat by the roadside and cried out, 'Death, come and take me!'
At that very moment, Death appeared before him.
'Why have you summoned me?' asked Death.
The Old Man looked at him and grew afraid.
'I did not mean now," he said. 'I have duties yet to perform.'
And he hurried away, leaving Death standing in the dust.
moral: Fear of death is stronger than weariness of life.
